text.title: Solo Multi-Product Portfolio Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Solo Multi-Product Portfolio Management application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Assign explicit weekly hours per product. Sum < 40h. Without explicit blocks, attention defaults to newest product.
rule.rationale: Attention is the scarce input; without budget, newest product cannibalizes maintenance of older ones.

rule.statement: Each product has explicit kill criteria reviewed quarterly: MRR < $X for 6 months OR user count declining 3 consecutive quarters. Below = kill or transfer.
rule.rationale: Sunk-cost is the silent killer of solo portfolios; explicit criteria prevent indefinite zombies.

rule.statement: Products share an audience channel (newsletter, X account, community). Cross-promote sparingly (≤1 mention / 4 weeks per channel).
rule.rationale: Audience leverage is the moat of solo portfolios; over-promotion = unsubscribe spike.

rule.statement: Products share stack components where possible (auth, billing, design system). Different stacks per product multiplies maintenance load.
rule.rationale: 5 stacks across 5 products = 5× security updates; consolidation cuts maintenance 50-70%.

rule.statement: Quarterly review: MRR / hour-spent per product. Lowest-ratio product gets reduced time OR killed. Highest-ratio gets more time.
rule.rationale: Time-allocation should follow evidence, not enthusiasm; quarterly review is the rebalance.
